title: tty ioctls on plain files return ENOSYS - prefer ENOTTY
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If you run TTY ioctls on a plain file, podman fails with ENOSYS instead of the more typical ENOTTY. I discovered this while running the Perl test suite inside a container. `ext/POSIX/t/termios.t` expects that exact errno (with some justification, I think).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
% cat termios-errno.c
#include <errno.h>
#include <fcntl.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#include <sys/types.h>
#include <termios.h>
#include <unistd.h>

int main(int argc, char* argv[]) {
    puts("Starting\n");
    int ret = tcdrain(STDOUT_FILENO);
    printf("tcdrain(stdout) returned %d\n", ret);
    if (ret) printf("%s\n", strerror(errno));

    int rfd = open(argv[0], O_RDONLY);
    ret = tcdrain(rfd);
    printf("tcdrain(%s) returned %d\n", argv[0], ret);
    if (ret) printf("%s\n", strerror(errno));

    return -ret;
}

% gcc -static -Wall -o termios-errno termios-errno.c
% podman build --tag termios-errno -f - .
FROM scratch
ADD termios-errno /
CMD ["/termios-errno"]
[^D]
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
% podman run --rm -t termios-errno
Starting

tcdrain(stdout) returned 0
tcdrain(/termios-errno) returned -1
Function not implemented
```

You get similar results with tcflush, tcsendbreak, etc.

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
% ./termios-errno
Starting

tcdrain(stdout) returned 0
tcdrain(./termios-errno) returned -1
Inappropriate ioctl for device
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.9
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
podman/stable,now 3.0.1+dfsg1-3+b2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

No to the latest version, yes to the troubleshooting guide. I've done my best to mitigate the version difference by checking issues to see if this might have been reported and fixed already, and didn't see anything.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical hardware booting Debian natively.

comments.body:
@brettcs I can confirm this is with older versions of podman and fixed in recent versions. I was able to reproduce this with `v.3.0.1` but not able to reproduce this in latest versions. I'd suggest you to update your podman version and try this again. Marking this as resolved. Please re-open if you face this issue with latest versions as well.

```console
[fl@fedora bin]$ vi term.c
[fl@fedora bin]$ gcc -static -Wall -o term term.c
[fl@fedora bin]$ vi Dockerfile
[fl@fedora bin]$ ./podman build -t term .
STEP 1/3: FROM scratch
STEP 2/3: ADD term /
--> 70dec48fc91
STEP 3/3: CMD ["/term"]
COMMIT term
--> 953f6bd1e27
Successfully tagged localhost/term:latest
953f6bd1e2799940832528a91f6e7f1184513cdd4bcbef825ee0d4316a7fb421
[fl@fedora bin]$ ./podman run --rm -t term
Starting

tcdrain(stdout) returned 0
tcdrain(/term) returned -1
Inappropriate ioctl for device
```
